Manufactured by [[Sienar Fleet Systems/Legends|Sienar Fleet Systems]],<ref name="DB" /> '''Obsidian 7''' was the designation of a [[TIE/ln space superiority starfighter/Legends|TIE/ln starfighter]] serving the [[Imperial Navy/Legends|Imperial Navy's]] [[Obsidian Squadron/Legends|Obsidian Squadron]]<ref name="CCG" /> by [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]].<ref name="NEC">''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''</ref> Modified for upper [[Atmosphere/Legends|atmosphere]] pursuits<ref name="BtM">''[[Star Wars: Behind the Magic]]''</ref> and maneuvers, Obsidian 7 was operated by a [[TIE pilot]] nicknamed "[[Winged Gundark]]" for<ref name="CCG" /> an impressive number of career [[Death/Legends|kills]]<ref name="CSWE">{{CSWECite|II|402|Obsidian Squadron}}</ref> in high-altitude [[Starfighter combat/Legends|battles]]<ref name="CCG" /> by 3 [[Galactic Standard Calendar|ABY]].<ref name="NEC" />

During that [[Standard year/Legends|year]], the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] [[Occupation of Bespin|took over]] the planet [[Bespin/Legends|Bespin]], with the intent to capture the [[YT-1300 light freighter/Legends|YT-1300 light freighter]] [[Millennium Falcon/Legends|''Millennium Falcon'']]. When the ''Millennium Falcon'' subsequently escaped from Bespin's [[Cloud City/Legends|Cloud City]],<ref name="NEC" /> Obsidian 7 was among the [[TIE series/Legends|TIE]] [[Starfighter/Legends|fighters]] that pursued the [[Freighter/Legends|freighter]] into [[Realspace/Legends|space]].<ref name="BtM" /> However, the squadron was unsuccessful in its pursuit, with the ''Millennium Falcon'' evading Imperial capture. Obsidian 7 was armed with a pair of [[L-s1 laser cannon/Legends|L-s1 laser cannons]].<ref name="ESB" />

==Behind the scenes==
The Obsidian 7 name was coined by [[Decipher]]'s [[1997]] ''[[Cloud City Limited]]'' expansion set of the [[Star Wars Customizable Card Game|''Star Wars'' Customizable Card Game]],<ref name="CCG" /> with the image being a rotated version of a TIE fighter previously pictured in [[1996]]'s ''[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game, Second Edition, Revised and Expanded]]''.<ref name="RPG">''[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game, Second Edition, Revised and Expanded]]''</ref> It was later mentioned in [[1998]]'s ''[[Star Wars: Behind the Magic]]'', where it was further linked to one of the TIE fighters seen in the [[1980]] [[original trilogy]] film [[Star Wars: Episode V The Empire Strikes Back|''Star Wars'': Episode V ''The Empire Strikes Back'']].<ref name="BtM" /> Before the film's [[May 21]], 1980 release,<ref name="Empire Turns 40">{{ThisWeek|6UccH037VQ0|The Empire Strikes Back Turns 40, Queen's Peril Gets a Voice, and More!|sw_url=video/the-empire-strikes-back-turns-40-queen-s-peril-gets-a-voice-and-more}}</ref> Obsidian 7 first appeared simultaneously in ''[[The Empire Strikes Back Storybook]]''<ref name="ESB storybook">''[[The Empire Strikes Back Storybook]]''</ref> and [[Star Wars: The Empire Strikes Back (novelization)|the film's novelization]],<ref name="ESB novel">[[Star Wars: The Empire Strikes Back (novelization)|''Star Wars Episode V: The Empire Strikes Back'' novelization]]</ref> both released [[April 12]] of the same year.<ref name="Amazon-RandomHouse">{{Amazon|url=Empire-Strikes-Back-Storybook/dp/0394844149|text=The Empire Strikes Back Storybook}} {{C|US hardcover}}</ref><ref name="Year by Year">''[[Star Wars Year By Year: A Visual History, Updated and Expanded Edition]]''</ref>
